mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP signals economic expansion, raising inflation expectations and real yields, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annualized growth rate is positive but the 10-year yield falls over the next 20 trading days in more than 40% of periods, or if the Spearman rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent yield change is below 0.10 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 6.0% indicates a slack labor market, reinforcing safe-haven demand for Treasuries and exerting downward pressure on the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 6% but the 10-year yield rises in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward, or if the average 20-day yield change after trigger is positive and significantly different from zero (t-test p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are elevated and the federal funds rate is near zero, it signals strong credit demand combined with accommodative monetary policy, which jointly boost growth expectations and drive long-term yields higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If when both conditions hold, the 60-day yield change is negative in more than half of instances, or the interaction term (HOUST * FEDFUNDS) in a regression does not have a statistically significant positive coefficient at the 5% level over a 5-year historical period.

mechanism_narrative: In an accommodative monetary policy regime (federal funds rate below 2%), any positive surprise in nonfarm payrolls signals future rate normalization, which raises term premiums and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS is below 2%, the correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and subsequent 5-day yield change is less than 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or the sign flips to negative in more than 30% of sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity, reducing recession fears and increasing inflation expectations, which drives the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO exceeds the trigger but the 5-day yield change is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average yield change after trigger is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment gains and rising consumer prices indicate an overheating economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy sooner than expected, which compresses term premiums and pushes the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150,000 and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 2.5%
failure_condition: If the joint trigger fires but the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the interaction term (PAYEMS * CPIAUCSL) does not show a statistically significant negative coefficient in a regression of yield changes over a 5-year history.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over several months signals economic weakness, increasing safe-haven demand for Treasuries and pushing the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rolling 3-month change in INDPRO is negative but the 20-day yield rises in more than 40% of periods, or the rank correlation between INDPRO change and subsequent yield change is above -0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in retail sales relative to its recent trend signals a consumer-led slowdown, increasing recession odds and driving the 10-year yield lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS falls below its 12-month MA but the 60-day yield change is positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the average subsequent yield change is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year walk-forward.
